title: eriogonum deflexum subsp. watsonii
intro: Eriogonum deflexum subsp. watsonii, also known as Watson's buckwheat or salsola, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and pain, treating wounds and skin conditions, and supporting immune function. Eriogonum deflexum subsp. watsonii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for various herbal preparations to be made for medicinal use.
